rationale: In the citing paper, Naylor et al. (2011) appears as a single supporting citation for the general claim that online reviews involve a relatively impoverished social context. The citation is used in discussion-level positioning rather than to build hypotheses, specify mechanisms, or guide methods in the profanity model. There is no clear evidence that the citing study extends, refines, applies, or challenges the cited paper's reviewer-ambiguity and egocentric-anchoring framework.
